governing_fact: Every Tesserine surface is a typeset record: the quiet, documentary projection of a machine-readable artifact that remains the product. (design/v1/SYSTEM.md)

members.role: the surface's identity line, masthead only
members.derivation: Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): the scale's top, 1.25^4 — the record's name outranks everything on the surface and nothing outranks it; leading tightest because an identity line is read as a mark, not a passage.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): 1.25^3 — the first level of the record's interior structure, one ratio step below the identity it belongs to.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): 1.25^2 — the second interior level, ranked by the same ratio; a record's structure descends evenly or it is not measured.

members.role: a minor subhead or the lede's standfirst
members.derivation: Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): 1.25^1 — the last rank above running matter, where the record's summary and minor groupings sit, one step off the reading base.

Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): the base itself — the record is read at length, so the whole scale is anchored on the reading size, and its 1.5 leading defines the rhythm line every space token is named from.

Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): 1.25^-1 — the one step below reading size, for the record's caption-grade matter; it keeps the full reading leading because provenance is still read, not skimmed.
